title: Build dev-to-prod parity compiler report
description:
Smart-goal bead for idea #3 from the 2026-05-12 idea-wizard pass: build a dev-to-prod parity compiler for Skillbox.

The idea: turn the roadmap's prod-bridge thesis into an executable `parity-report`/planner surface that compares client overlay services, env files, health checks, reverse-proxy roles, deploy-skill modes, and production-stack declarations. The output should name exact missing pieces and generate reviewable preview artifacts, so Skillbox becomes a staging-grade proving layer without turning into a hosted platform or replacing app-specific CI/deploy systems.
design:
repo_state:
  date: 2026-05-12
  head: 0691013 fix(skills): prefer path-specific scope rules
  branch: main ahead origin/main by 6 at bead creation time
  dirty_paths_at_creation: .beads/issues.jsonl, .env-manager/runtime_manager/cli.py, .gitignore, README.md, scripts/sbp, tests/test_cli_wrappers.py
context:
  source_idea: idea-wizard ranked idea #3, Dev-to-prod parity compiler
  docs_anchor: docs/ROADMAP.md Phase 2 says Skillbox is missing the bridge to the operator's nginx/deploy layer, including reverse-proxy parity, env parity, and deploy skill integration.
  implemented_surface: runtime graph, local runtime profiles, parity ledger validation, env files, client-project/open/publish, stewardship-report, and agent-readable capabilities already exist.
  open_beads: skillbox-agm broad audit/smart-goal bead; skillbox-o7j MCP extras review bead.
integrity_gap:
  The README and roadmap describe parity concepts, but there is no single inspectable command that answers whether a client overlay is actually ready to behave like a staging/devbox tier for a real prod stack.
hardening_decision:
  hardening-first by extension: build on runtime graph, parity ledger, env files, client projection, and stewardship-report instead of inventing a new deployment subsystem.
out_of_scope:
  - Do not build a hosted distributor, hosted control plane, browser IDE, or generic deploy product.
  - Do not mutate production infrastructure.
  - Do not require live nginx, registry, cloud, or Tailscale credentials for the default proof path.
  - Do not hide app-specific deploy decisions inside Skillbox; generate declarations, reports, and safe previews.
acceptance_criteria:
/goal $domain-planner $describe $reproduce process complete

One-shot goal: Design and implement the first Skillbox dev-to-prod parity compiler slice: a read-only parity report/planner that compares declared client runtime surfaces against a production-stack contract and reports reverse-proxy, env, healthcheck, and deploy-mode drift with machine-readable JSON, concise text output, and regression proof.

Success criteria:
- Start from the existing runtime graph, parity ledger, env_files, service healthcheck, local runtime mode, client projection, and stewardship-report concepts; do not create a separate deployment platform.
- Define a minimal production-stack contract that can live in a client overlay or adjacent private config and can represent reverse-proxy routes/upstreams, env var expectations, health endpoints, deploy skill modes, and optional Docker network assumptions.
- Add a read-only CLI surface with JSON output and safe first-try guidance, tentatively `python3 .env-manager/manage.py parity-report <client> --format json`, plus an MCP/tooling path if it fits existing runtime-manager patterns.
- The report must classify `ready`, `missing`, `drift`, `deferred`, and `not_assessed` domains; each finding must include the source declaration, the expected prod contract, and the next safe command or file to edit.
- Include at least one fixture client that proves the happy path and at least one fixture that proves drift across proxy, env, healthcheck, and deploy-mode domains.
- Integrate the summary into `stewardship-report` or explicitly document why the first slice keeps it separate.
- Update README/docs/ROADMAP so implemented behavior, limitations, and non-goals are separated from future roadmap prose.
- Verification must include focused unit tests for the parity planner/report, CLI JSON/text behavior, fixture drift cases, `git diff --check`, `make doctor`, and `python3 -m unittest discover -s tests` unless a concrete environment blocker is recorded.
- Detached fresh-eyes review is required before completion, and this goal authorizes at least one scoped detached reviewer subagent for that gate, plus additional detached review rounds as needed: launch or route an independent reviewer that did not implement the work, have it inspect the final diff/artifacts/validation evidence, and address or explicitly block on its findings before marking the goal complete.
- Completion means validate -> commit only when authorized -> post-commit reality check; if commit authorization, credentials, or external prod fixtures block closeout, report the exact resume condition rather than calling it done.
notes:
chain_context: Builds on the idea-wizard #3 recommendation and the existing Skillbox roadmap, while avoiding duplication of the closed agent-ergonomics/SBP/MCP goal beads.
modes: no overlay-backed modes report was present for this repo at creation time.
crap_status: unknown fresh baseline for the future parity-report implementation; run CRAP only if the implementation touches enough supported Python hotspot scope to make the score meaningful.
skill_combo: domain-planner to shape the contract, describe to pin pass/fail cases, reproduce for command-first verification.
first_step: read docs/ROADMAP.md Phase 2 plus the current runtime graph/env_files/parity-ledger validation code, then draft the smallest production-stack contract fixture before coding the report.
loop_goal: make Skillbox able to prove whether a client devbox surface matches the operator's prod-stack shape closely enough to be a staging-grade proving layer.
success_criteria_summary: read-only report/planner, explicit prod contract, drift classification, fixture coverage, docs update, focused and broad validation, detached review, commit/reality-check gate.
resume_condition: start implementation by defining the production-stack contract shape and fixture matrix; another /smart pass is useful after the report exists or if contract placement becomes ambiguous.
completion_gate: pending; this is a goal contract, not an implementation completion claim.
smart_goal_mode: compact-goal
smart_goal_contract: |
  /goal $domain-planner $describe $reproduce process complete
  
  One-shot goal: Design and implement the first Skillbox dev-to-prod parity compiler slice: a read-only parity report/planner that compares declared client runtime surfaces against a production-stack contract and reports reverse-proxy, env, healthcheck, and deploy-mode drift with machine-readable JSON, concise text output, and regression proof.
  
  Success criteria:
  - Start from the existing runtime graph, parity ledger, env_files, service healthcheck, local runtime mode, client projection, and stewardship-report concepts; do not create a separate deployment platform.
  - Define a minimal production-stack contract that can live in a client overlay or adjacent private config and can represent reverse-proxy routes/upstreams, env var expectations, health endpoints, deploy skill modes, and optional Docker network assumptions.
  - Add a read-only CLI surface with JSON output and safe first-try guidance, tentatively `python3 .env-manager/manage.py parity-report <client> --format json`, plus an MCP/tooling path if it fits existing runtime-manager patterns.
  - The report must classify `ready`, `missing`, `drift`, `deferred`, and `not_assessed` domains; each finding must include the source declaration, the expected prod contract, and the next safe command or file to edit.
  - Include at least one fixture client that proves the happy path and at least one fixture that proves drift across proxy, env, healthcheck, and deploy-mode domains.
  - Integrate the summary into `stewardship-report` or explicitly document why the first slice keeps it separate.
  - Update README/docs/ROADMAP so implemented behavior, limitations, and non-goals are separated from future roadmap prose.
  - Verification must include focused unit tests for the parity planner/report, CLI JSON/text behavior, fixture drift cases, `git diff --check`, `make doctor`, and `python3 -m unittest discover -s tests` unless a concrete environment blocker is recorded.
  - Detached fresh-eyes review is required before completion, and this goal authorizes at least one scoped detached reviewer subagent for that gate, plus additional detached review rounds as needed: launch or route an independent reviewer that did not implement the work, have it inspect the final diff/artifacts/validation evidence, and address or explicitly block on its findings before marking the goal complete.
  - Completion means validate -> commit only when authorized -> post-commit reality check; if commit authorization, credentials, or external prod fixtures block closeout, report the exact resume condition rather than calling it done.
close_reason: Implemented and committed as 6eef967; remaining broad validation blockers recorded as local-state/environmental.

comments.author: b
comments.text: Implementation evidence 2026-05-12: added read-only parity-report CLI/MCP surface, production_stack overlay contract preservation, dev/prod parity summary in stewardship-report, docs updates, and fixture regressions for ready, drift, no-contract, cwd inference, MCP input, and service_mode_commands client scoping. Detached reviewer 019e1e51-c203-7201-bb1e-e8dc0f13470b found four issues; all addressed. Verification: py_compile targeted files; focused parity/CLI/MCP/runtime-model tests; real parity-report personal --format json (ready=false, status=not_ready, blocking_count=0, contract_present=false for current private overlay); git diff --check; make doctor; make dev-sanity (existing skill-repo-install warning only); python3 -m unittest discover -s tests => 926 tests, 1 skipped. Closeout blocker: commit/post-commit reality check still requires explicit commit authorization.

comments.author: b
comments.text: Closeout after current run: committed implementation as 6eef967 feat(runtime): add parity reporting. Refreshed evidence: git diff --check passed; py_compile targeted runtime files passed; focused unittest lane tests.test_runtime_manager tests.test_cli_wrappers tests.test_mcp_server tests.test_mmdx_open tests.test_runtime_hotspot_units tests.test_cli_units passed earlier in this run (353 tests); R-001/R-002 agent ergonomics regressions passed after R-002 expectation update. Current broad blockers are local-state/environmental: make doctor fails only on skill-repo-install stale installed default skills in .skillbox-state for divide-and-conquer/domain-planner/domain-reviewer/domain-scaffolder; full unittest discover hangs in install.sh --verify -> manage.py first-box personal and was killed. No remaining tracked implementation diff for parity-report.

title: Review Skillbox repo Claude-only MCP extras cm dcg fwc
description:
Audit finding from sbp mcp in opensource/skillbox: expected servers are skillbox and skillbox-operator, but .mcp.json also exposes Claude-only cm, dcg, and fwc while Codex config does not. This is not a pdsmvp blocker, but it is repo-local MCP parity drift that should be reviewed deliberately.

Scope:
- Inspect .mcp.json and .codex/config.toml plus the runtime/skillbox overlay expectations.
- Decide whether cm/dcg/fwc are intentional Claude-only local extras, should be mirrored to Codex, or should be removed/disabled.
- Do not broaden into global/fleet MCP cleanup.

Acceptance:
- Decision is recorded with file evidence.
- If config changes are made, sbp mcp --format json and sbp recalibrate report the intended parity state.
- make dev-sanity or focused MCP tests run if a runtime surface changes.
close_reason: Reviewed and recorded decision: cm/dcg/fwc are ignored Claude-local extras; no tracked MCP config change needed.

comments.author: b
comments.text: Decision: keep cm/dcg/fwc as ignored Claude-local/operator extras, not tracked Codex parity requirements. Evidence: sbp mcp expected_servers are skillbox and skillbox-operator; both Claude/Codex have those, and only .mcp.json has cm/dcg/fwc. .mcp.json and .codex/config.toml are ignored local config. The cm/dcg/fwc launch binaries are absent under /home/sandbox/.local/bin and /Users/b/.local/bin in this run. README/runtime model documents cm/fwc/dcg as optional profile/surface additions rather than required project MCP parity. No config change made.

title: Run post-SBP reality check and smart goal
description:
After the SBP visibility/MCP recalibration bead is closed, run a repo-vs-vision reality check followed by a `$smart goal` pass from this repo root.

Prompt to run:
`$reality-check-for-project + $smart goal`

The point is not a brainstorm. Compare implemented code, docs, plans, Beads, and validation commands against the project vision; then write the single most accretive smart-goal contract for the next move. If the pass discovers truly accretive follow-up work, create/update Beads with evidence and dependencies.
design:
concern: post-visibility project truth and next smart goal
writes: Beads issue notes/children and overlay-backed smart-chain artifacts if the repo has a matching invocation root
non_goals: broad brainstorm; implementation before the reality check names the next move
validation: repo-native validation named by the smart goal, plus Beads dependency/state check
acceptance_criteria:
- The SBP recalibration bead is closed or its unresolved blocker is explicitly accepted as not blocking this repo assessment.
- `$reality-check-for-project + $smart goal` was run from the repo root with repo evidence, not vibes.
- The reality check distinguishes implemented state from roadmap/planned state.
- The smart goal names loop_goal, success criteria, resume condition, and validation/commit/reality-check gate.
- Any new follow-up Beads are evidence-backed, dependency-linked, and not duplicates.
- Repo-native validation commands are named or exact blockers are recorded.
notes: Backlog refresh 2026-05-11T23:06:38.046442+00:00: confirmed this repo should have this operational bead.
close_reason: post-SBP reality check and smart-goal contract recorded in Beads

comments.author: b
comments.text:
Reality check 2026-05-11: README/VISION promise is one private Tailnet/Docker dev box with durable state, explicit runtime graphs, repo overlays, agent context, skill visibility, MCP/operator tools, Swimmers integration, worker/session history, and low ceremony. Current implemented proof: `make doctor` passed 13/0/0; `make dev-sanity` passed 23 checks with 1 warning; SBP repo-local skill view is clean with 0 missing_for_cwd, 0 scope_violations, 0 broken_project; MCP expected `skillbox` and `skillbox-operator` are present in Claude and Codex; `manage.py capabilities --json` exposes `swimmers-launch`; `manage.py swimmers-launch ... --dry-run --format json` and `scripts/sbp launch ... --dry-run --json` both produced parseable dry-run payloads; focused `python3 -m unittest tests.test_swimmers_launch tests.test_cli_units tests.test_cli_wrappers tests.test_mcp_visibility` ran 40 tests OK; `git diff --check` passed. Current gaps: current Swimmers launch surface is uncommitted and only dry-run proven, no non-dry-run POST was attempted to avoid spawning agents; full `python3 -m unittest discover -s tests` is not a clean proof because it hung in `tests/test_install_script.py::test_verify_runs_post_install_checks` inside `install.sh --verify -> manage.py first-box`; `make dev-sanity` still warns about extra default/global installed skills (`cass`, `cass-memory`, `changelog-md-workmanship`, `ga4`, `readme-writing`, `smart`) which I treated as out-of-scope global drift; ignored local MCP extras `cm`, `dcg`, `fwc` remain operator-local review items; live DigitalOcean/Tailscale provisioning proof is still external. No overlay plan index or released plan tree exists under the resolved skillbox client overlay, so Beads are the active plan surface. Existing broad Pass 2 smart goal remains directionally right, but the concrete next hardening gate is full-suite determinism before any claim that the new agent surface is complete.

Smart goal contract:
/goal $reproduce $installer-workmanship process complete

One-shot goal: Make Skillbox full repo validation deterministic again by diagnosing and fixing the `python3 -m unittest discover -s tests` hang at `tests/test_install_script.py::test_verify_runs_post_install_checks` / `install.sh --verify -> manage.py first-box`, then rerun the current Swimmers batch-launch surface closeout from a clean full-suite baseline.

Success criteria:
- Reproduce the install verification hang with a bounded command and capture where `install.sh`, `first-box`, or acceptance is waiting.
- Fix the root cause without weakening real installer verification; if the test needs a tighter harness path, prove production `--verify` behavior remains meaningful.
- Preserve current repo-local SBP/MCP state and do not prune broad global/default skill drift as part of this goal.
- Keep the Swimmers launch surface scoped: `manage.py swimmers-launch`, `scripts/sbp launch/bulk`, README docs, capabilities, and focused tests stay green.
- Focused tests pass: `python3 -m unittest tests.test_swimmers_launch tests.test_cli_units tests.test_cli_wrappers tests.test_mcp_visibility tests.test_install_script`.
- Broad proof passes or blocks explicitly: `python3 -m unittest discover -s tests`, `make doctor`, `make dev-sanity`, and `git diff --check`.
- Completion gate is validate -> commit only with explicit authorization -> post-commit reality check; absent commit authorization, report commit-blocked rather than complete.
- Detached fresh-eyes review is required before completion and may be a scoped reviewer pass on the final diff and validation evidence; do not launch one per bead.

Loop goal: restore trust in Skillbox validation and make the current agent-facing Swimmers launch slice closeable.
Resume condition: start the next implementation pass by reproducing the install verification hang with a bounded command; another `/smart` pass is useful only after full-suite determinism is restored or the blocker is proven external.
Completion gate: pending.

title: Run SBP skill and MCP visibility recalibration
description:
Run a repo-local `$sbp` recalibration from this repo root before more agent work relies on skill or MCP visibility.

Required read-only pass:
- `sbp`
- `sbp recalibrate`
- `sbp skills --issues-only --no-global --format json`
- `sbp mcp --format json`
- `sbp beads status`

Classify any `missing_for_cwd`, `scope_violations`, `broken_project`, MCP missing/extra/parity drift, and Beads drift. Use narrow dry-runs before applying any sync/remove/prune action. Prefer repo-local fixes; do not do broad global cleanup unless the evidence is specific and recorded here.
design:
concern: Skillbox visibility and MCP parity
writes: project-local skill links, `.mcp.json`, `.codex/config.toml`, repo-local Beads notes only when justified by dry-run evidence
non_goals: broad global pruning; making task skills global to unblock a single run
validation: sbp recalibrate; sbp mcp --format json; sbp beads status
acceptance_criteria:
- SBP read-only commands above were run from the repo root and summarized in issue notes.
- Add/remove/sync decisions are classified by mismatch type and policy reason.
- Any mutation used a reviewed dry-run first, then the narrow apply command only.
- `sbp recalibrate` and `sbp mcp --format json` were rerun after changes, or exact blockers were recorded.
- Remaining human decisions, if any, are explicit.
notes: Backlog refresh 2026-05-11T23:06:37.950786+00:00: confirmed this repo should have this operational bead.
close_reason: SBP skill/MCP recalibration evidence recorded; no narrow mutation needed

comments.author: b
comments.text: Evidence 2026-05-11: ran required read-only pass from repo root: `sbp`, `sbp recalibrate`, `sbp skills --issues-only --no-global --format json`, `sbp mcp --format json`, and `sbp beads status`. Skill visibility is clean: 21 effective skills, 29 occurrences, 0 missing_for_cwd, 0 scope_violations, 0 broken_project, 0 shadowed, 0 global/local issues in no-global repo view. Beads status is initialized at `.beads/`, database `.beads/beads.db`, jsonl `.beads/issues.jsonl`, 4 total issues, 2 open, 1 blocked, 1 ready. MCP audit expected servers are `skillbox` and `skillbox-operator`; both Claude and Codex have those, no missing or invalid config. Remaining review item: Claude-only ignored local MCP extras `cm`, `dcg`, `fwc`; these are not tracked repo files (`.mcp.json` and `.codex/` ignored), binaries were absent at `/home/sandbox/.local/bin/*` and `/Users/b/.local/bin/*`, and README documents these as opened/client/core/connector surfaces, so I did not mirror broken Codex entries or prune local Claude config. `make doctor` passed 13 checks, 0 warnings, 0 failed. No mutation was needed beyond this Beads evidence update.

title: Run Skillbox agent-ergonomics Pass 2 across CLI/MCP surfaces
description: Goal contract for Skillbox agent-ergonomics Pass 2: harden the full CLI/MCP surface beyond manage.py with applied, regression-pinned improvements.
design:
repo_state:
  repo_head: 22519dd
  compared_to: skillbox-smart-20260511-sbp-mcp-closeout-fbs
  branch: main
repo_signals:
  prior_pass: agent_ergonomics_audit Pass 1 added manage.py capabilities, robot-docs, robot-triage, JSON aliases, and unknown-command hints.
  current_verification: make doctor passed 13 checks; focused tests `python3 -m unittest tests.test_cli_units tests.test_mcp_server` passed 40 tests; R-001 audit regression passed; git diff --check passed.
  current_surfaces: capabilities payload exposes manage.py contract; sbp/sbo help exists but sbo identifies itself as sbp; box.py and 04-reconcile.py help are argparse-only; operator MCP parity has expected skillbox servers plus Claude-only extras.
  local_state: worktree has pre-existing untracked .buildooor only.
integrity_gap:
  Pass 1 hardened the primary runtime CLI, but adjacent operator, wrapper, reconcile, and lifecycle surfaces still have uneven agent-readable contracts and weaker regression-pinned ergonomics.
hardening_decision:
  hardening-first; extend and regression-pin existing CLI/MCP surfaces before inventing new workflow features.
loop_goal:
  Make Skillbox's agent-facing command surface first-try and self-documenting across the daily operator/runtime loop, with measured Pass 2 uplift and post-commit proof.
acceptance_criteria:
/goal $agent-ergonomics-and-intuitiveness-maximization-for-cli-tools $divide-and-conquer $reproduce process complete

One-shot goal: Resume `agent_ergonomics_audit/` as Skillbox agent-ergonomics Pass 2 and harden the full agent-facing CLI/MCP surface, not just `.env-manager/manage.py`: `scripts/04-reconcile.py`, `scripts/box.py`, `scripts/sbp`, `scripts/sbo`, operator MCP tools, and the distribution/client lifecycle commands should become first-try, machine-readable, safe-by-default surfaces with regression proof.

Success criteria:
- Start on current `main`; do not create a branch or sibling audit workspace; preserve pre-existing unrelated local state such as `.buildooor/` unless the pass deliberately scopes it in.
- Treat Pass 1 as the baseline: `manage.py capabilities --json`, `robot-docs guide`, `--robot-triage`, JSON typo aliases, and unknown-command suggestions stay green and pinned.
- Run the agent-ergonomics full/resume flow in `agent_ergonomics_audit/`, with surface inventory and evidence-backed scoring for `manage.py`, `04-reconcile.py`, `box.py`, `sbp`, `sbo`, operator MCP tools, and safe installer/release help or dry-run surfaces.
- Ship a substantive Pass 2 batch, not a report-only pass: at least 5 high-leverage applied recommendations, or explicitly run the verbatim Ambition Bar self-prompt and document why fewer is the true convergence point.
- Include at least one self-documenting machine contract where missing (`capabilities --json`, `robot-docs guide`, or `--robot-triage` equivalent), one JSON/robot read-side improvement, one exact-command error rewrite, one intent-inference or alias fix, and one safety/dry-run pedagogy improvement if the scored surfaces expose those gaps.
- Pin every applied recommendation with focused regression tests under the repo or `agent_ergonomics_audit/audit/regression_tests/`, including stdout/stderr split, deterministic JSON, non-TTY behavior, and destructive-operation safe alternatives where relevant.
- Verify with current repo-native gates: focused CLI/MCP tests, existing audit regression tests, `make doctor`, `git diff --check`, and broaden to `python3 -m unittest discover -s tests` before completion unless a concrete environment blocker is recorded.
- Run a fresh-context canonical-task simulation after the changes; transcripts should prove a new agent can discover status, doctor, box dry-run/status, SBP skill/MCP recalibration, and client/distribution preview commands without external docs.
- Detached fresh-eyes review is required before completion, and this goal authorizes at least one scoped detached reviewer subagent for that gate, plus additional detached review rounds as needed: launch or route an independent reviewer that did not implement the work, have it inspect the final diff/artifacts/validation evidence, and address or explicitly block on its findings before marking the goal complete.
- Completion means validate -> commit the coherent Pass 2 code and audit artifacts on `main` -> run a post-commit reality check; if commit, credentials, missing toolchains, or destructive infrastructure proof blocks closeout, report the exact resume condition instead of calling it done.
notes:
chain_context: Builds on prior closed smart link by moving from SBP/MCP visibility closeout to the next unclosed Pass 1 handoff item: full agent-ergonomics Pass 2.
modes: legacy repo-local modes report says the recurring risk is implicit runtime/agent contracts; this goal directly hardens those contracts.
crap_status: unknown fresh baseline for Pass 2 target scopes; run CRAP only if the implementation touches supported Python hotspots enough to make it meaningful.
skill_combo: agent-ergonomics-and-intuitiveness-maximization-for-cli-tools -> divide-and-conquer for multi-surface apply/review -> reproduce for command-first validation.
first_step: read agent_ergonomics_audit/audit/HANDOFF.md, then run the agent-ergonomics resume/full intake for Pass 2 against /Users/b/repos/opensource/skillbox.
resume_condition: another /smart pass is useful after Pass 2 has either committed and passed post-commit reality check, or blocked on an exact toolchain/credential/infrastructure condition.
completion_gate: pending; this is a goal contract, not an implementation completion claim.
smart_goal_mode: compact-goal
smart_goal_contract: |
  /goal $agent-ergonomics-and-intuitiveness-maximization-for-cli-tools $divide-and-conquer $reproduce process complete
  
  One-shot goal: Resume `agent_ergonomics_audit/` as Skillbox agent-ergonomics Pass 2 and harden the full agent-facing CLI/MCP surface, not just `.env-manager/manage.py`: `scripts/04-reconcile.py`, `scripts/box.py`, `scripts/sbp`, `scripts/sbo`, operator MCP tools, and the distribution/client lifecycle commands should become first-try, machine-readable, safe-by-default surfaces with regression proof.
  
  Success criteria:
  - Start on current `main`; do not create a branch or sibling audit workspace; preserve pre-existing unrelated local state such as `.buildooor/` unless the pass deliberately scopes it in.
  - Treat Pass 1 as the baseline: `manage.py capabilities --json`, `robot-docs guide`, `--robot-triage`, JSON typo aliases, and unknown-command suggestions stay green and pinned.
  - Run the agent-ergonomics full/resume flow in `agent_ergonomics_audit/`, with surface inventory and evidence-backed scoring for `manage.py`, `04-reconcile.py`, `box.py`, `sbp`, `sbo`, operator MCP tools, and safe installer/release help or dry-run surfaces.
  - Ship a substantive Pass 2 batch, not a report-only pass: at least 5 high-leverage applied recommendations, or explicitly run the verbatim Ambition Bar self-prompt and document why fewer is the true convergence point.
  - Include at least one self-documenting machine contract where missing (`capabilities --json`, `robot-docs guide`, or `--robot-triage` equivalent), one JSON/robot read-side improvement, one exact-command error rewrite, one intent-inference or alias fix, and one safety/dry-run pedagogy improvement if the scored surfaces expose those gaps.
  - Pin every applied recommendation with focused regression tests under the repo or `agent_ergonomics_audit/audit/regression_tests/`, including stdout/stderr split, deterministic JSON, non-TTY behavior, and destructive-operation safe alternatives where relevant.
  - Verify with current repo-native gates: focused CLI/MCP tests, existing audit regression tests, `make doctor`, `git diff --check`, and broaden to `python3 -m unittest discover -s tests` before completion unless a concrete environment blocker is recorded.
  - Run a fresh-context canonical-task simulation after the changes; transcripts should prove a new agent can discover status, doctor, box dry-run/status, SBP skill/MCP recalibration, and client/distribution preview commands without external docs.
  - Detached fresh-eyes review is required before completion, and this goal authorizes at least one scoped detached reviewer subagent for that gate, plus additional detached review rounds as needed: launch or route an independent reviewer that did not implement the work, have it inspect the final diff/artifacts/validation evidence, and address or explicitly block on its findings before marking the goal complete.
  - Completion means validate -> commit the coherent Pass 2 code and audit artifacts on `main` -> run a post-commit reality check; if commit, credentials, missing toolchains, or destructive infrastructure proof blocks closeout, report the exact resume condition instead of calling it done.

comments.author: b
comments.text: Post-SBP reality update 2026-05-11: current worktree has a partial Swimmers batch-launch implementation (`manage.py swimmers-launch` plus `sbp launch/bulk`) with focused tests and dry-run proof, but broad completion is blocked by full-suite determinism. `python3 -m unittest discover -s tests` hung in `tests/test_install_script.py::test_verify_runs_post_install_checks` via `install.sh --verify -> manage.py first-box`; next hardening goal is to reproduce and fix that validation lane before claiming Pass 2 or the Swimmers launch slice complete.

title: Repair repo-local skill drift, then close SBP/MCP visibility slice
description:
Hardening-first move: repair the repo-local agent-ergonomics skill symlinks that `sbp recalibrate` reports as broken, rerun the live SBP/MCP audit surfaces, then commit the current SBP/MCP visibility slice with the passing test proof.

Why now: the broad code slice is already verified by focused and full unittest runs plus `make doctor`; the remaining trust gap is that the new visibility tooling still reports live repo-local drift in the very repo it is meant to calibrate.
design:
repo_state:
  repo_head: 2641d87 refactor(box): lower lifecycle hotspot risk
  compared_to: first beads-backed smart link in this repo; legacy markdown chain ignored except as drift evidence
  branch: main; ahead origin/main by 1; worktree contains broad uncommitted SBP/MCP visibility slice
repo_signals:
  changed_paths: .env-manager/runtime_manager/skill_visibility.py, .env-manager/runtime_manager/mcp_visibility.py, .env-manager/runtime_manager/cli.py, scripts/sbp, README.md, Makefile, runtime model/context/validation, MCP/CLI/skill tests, agent_ergonomics_audit, skill-mapping plan
  verification: focused 89 tests OK; full unittest discover 900 tests OK, 1 skipped; make doctor 13 passed; git diff --check OK
  live_audit: sbp recalibrate reports 2 broken project-local links and 1 missing cwd-scoped skill for agent-ergonomics-and-intuitiveness-maximization-for-cli-tools
  mcp_audit: expected skillbox and skillbox-operator present in Claude/Codex; Claude-only extras cm, dcg, fwc reported as review items
integrity_gap:
  The new visibility/recalibration feature works, but the local repo still has a broken required project skill link, so closeout should not claim the repo is calibrated until that link is repaired or intentionally pruned.
hardening_decision:
  hardening-first; close the reported repo-local drift before committing this agent-facing visibility slice.
loop_goal:
  Make Skillbox's SBP/MCP visibility control plane trustworthy enough that a fresh agent can run sbp recalibrate and get actionable repo-local state without broken local links masking the real policy output.
acceptance_criteria:
- `sbp recalibrate --limit 20` reports beads initialized and no broken project-local skill links for this repo.
- `python3 .env-manager/manage.py mcp-audit --cwd "$PWD" --format json` still has no missing expected servers for Claude or Codex; any Claude-only extras are either mirrored or explicitly accepted as local extras.
- `python3 -m unittest discover -s tests`, `make doctor`, and `git diff --check` pass after the local drift repair.
- A logical commit captures the SBP/MCP visibility slice without accidental secrets, backup files, or generated noise.
notes:
chain_context: no prior repo-local beads chain issue existed; memory/legacy chain showed recent runtime-contract, MCP/SBP, CLI ergonomics, and operator-trust work, but the current diff is a newer SBP/MCP visibility closeout.
modes: no modes report found under the resolved evaluation root.
crap_status: unknown baseline for current Python runtime visibility/CLI scope; full unittest and doctor proof exists, but no fresh CRAP score was run.
skill_combo: direct closeout; optional `sbp skill add agent-ergonomics-and-intuitiveness-maximization-for-cli-tools --cwd "$PWD"`, then rerun `sbp recalibrate`, `sbp mcp`, and commit.
first_step: python3 .env-manager/manage.py skill add agent-ergonomics-and-intuitiveness-maximization-for-cli-tools --cwd "$PWD"
resume_condition: rerun /smart after `sbp recalibrate` no longer reports broken project-local links and the coherent SBP/MCP visibility slice has been committed, or if the MCP extras cm/dcg/fwc turn out to be intentional and need a policy decision.
completion_gate: pending; this invocation is a recommendation, not an implementation closeout.
